seoTitle: Loxton North vs Lucky Bay — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Loxton North and Lucky Bay. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Loxton North vs Lucky Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Loxton North and Lucky Bay.
heading: Loxton North vs Lucky Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Loxton North, South Australia and Lucky Bay, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lucky Bay has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Loxton North (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Loxton North or Lucky Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Loxton North has a flood risk score of 58/100, while Lucky Bay scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Loxton North scores 51/100 for bushfire risk. Lucky Bay scores 27/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Loxton North or Lucky Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lucky Bay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.27% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
